commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f1b505648b8a39f6f107a7648a0331410dddd407)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Matrix direct-room evidence so a bot member event with `is_direct: false` returns `strict: false`, and updates the direct-room unit tests for explicit false and absent-flag behavior.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source-level bug surface, but not with live Matrix proof here. Current main shows the helper ignores local `is_direct:false` for `strict`, while the inbound path and direct-management tests show additional paths the PR must cover.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed because the external PR lacks real behavior proof and the remaining fix must align Matrix routing precedence across inbound, send-target, and direct-management contracts.

target.title: fix(matrix): honor is_direct=false on bot member event as strict-DM veto
target.body:
Fixes #85017.

## Summary

The matrix plugin's strict-DM classifier in `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/direct-room.ts` was a pure member-count check: any 2-joined-member room with the bot + one user was classified as a direct room, regardless of any other signal. `inspectMatrixDirectRoomEvidence` *did* read the `is_direct` flag from the bot's own `m.room.member` event via `hasDirectMatrixMemberFlag`, but the result only populated the informational `viaMemberState`/`memberStateFlag` fields â€” it never vetoed the classification.

Consequence: rooms intentionally created as groups (named, `is_direct: false`, listed under `channels.matrix.groups[<roomId>]` in config) are silently treated as DMs the moment they have exactly two members. DM mode then bypasses `requireMention` and the per-room/per-group skill scoping for those rooms. The workaround today is "add a third member", which makes the schema's `groups` vs `rooms` distinction broken for the smallest rooms and forces a placeholder user.

## Change

In `inspectMatrixDirectRoomEvidence`, after `hasDirectMatrixMemberFlag` returns, if the bot's own member event carries `is_direct: false` (explicit signal â€” either `/createRoom` was called with `is_direct: false` or the membership was rewritten by an admin), short-circuit and return `strict: false`. When `is_direct` is absent, the legacy count-based classification is preserved.

```ts
const memberStateFlag = await hasDirectMatrixMemberFlag(params.client, params.roomId, selfUserId);
if (memberStateFlag === false) {
  return {
    joinedMembers,
    strict: false,
    viaMemberState: false,
    memberStateFlag,
  };
}
```

The existing `records only the local member-state direct flag` test encoded the buggy behavior (asserting `strict: true` despite `is_direct: false`); it's rewritten to assert the new veto. A companion test pins down the no-signal path so legacy behavior is retained for rooms that have always been treated as DMs.

## Test plan

- [x] `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/direct-room.test.ts` updated and re-runs the two affected scenarios.
- [ ] CI `extensions/matrix` test suite green.
- [ ] Manual: create a room with `/createRoom` + `is_direct: false`, bot joins (2 members), confirm `requireMention: true` is honored at runtime (plain message ignored, `@bot ...` triggers reply).
- [ ] Manual: existing 2-person DMs without an `is_direct` field on member events continue to behave as DMs (no regression for the long-standing pattern).

## Related

- #57124 â€” earlier fix for the same root cause limited to "discovered DM candidates". This PR extends the intent to the strict-classification path that drives runtime mention/skill routing.
- #85017 â€” the filed issue with the full reproduction.

## Notes

Per CONTRIBUTING I haven't touched `CHANGELOG.md`. Happy to add tests for the runtime call sites that consume `isStrictDirectRoom` (e.g. `channel.runtime.ts`) if the maintainers want broader coverage in the same PR â€” flagging as a possible follow-up rather than scope-creeping here.

đź¤– Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
